project_overview.project_name: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Revenue Mobilization and Public Resource Management (P162302)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To increase the collection of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's own source revenues and improve the management of public resources
project_overview.beneficiaries: Government of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, provincial departments, local governments (TMAs), taxpayers, citizens of KP province

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - KP has transitioned out of vulnerability and crises over past 8 years but remains vulnerable. Integration of former FATA with KP creates new challenges. FATA is one of poorest regions of Pakistan with significant development needs. KP is highly vulnerable to climate change and hydro-meteorological disasters.

key_components.description: Results Area 1 - Efficient Revenue Mobilization: Expanding tax base, enhancing institutional capacity for tax collection, facilitating taxpayer compliance through automation and facilitation centers, mobilizing non-tax revenues from government real estate. Results Area 2 - Effective Public Resource Management: Consolidating government cash in TSA, strengthening local government expenditure accountability through NFMIS expansion, strengthening performance orientation through output-based budgeting, improving investment planning and ADP execution, increasing transparency in financial asset management.

key_components.component_name: Part 2: Investment Project Financing (IPF) - Technical Assistance and Capacity Building
key_components.description: Subcomponent 2.1: Foundational Support for e-Government Functionality (US$12.5M) - Business process mapping, automation of administrative processes in 7 departments, e-archiving, digitization, database integration for tax authorities, NFMIS extension to TMAs. Subcomponent 2.2: Program Coordination and Support (US$5.5M) - Establishment of Shared Services Unit (SSU) and Operations Support Unit (OSU), capacity building, communications, M&E, third-party verification.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Finance Department (FD) - Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, with support from: Excise, Taxation and Narcotics Control Department (ETNCD), Board of Revenue (BoR), KP Revenue Authority (KPRA), Planning and Development Department (P&DD), Science Technology and Information Technology (ST&IT), Local Government Department
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Reform Working Group (RWG) comprising officials from various government entities provides technical support and oversight. PFM Reforms Oversight Committee headed by Minister of Finance acts as Steering Committee. Operations Support Unit (OSU) headed by Operations Coordinator manages day-to-day implementation. Shared Services Unit (SSU) oversees financial management, procurement, and environmental/social safeguards.
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for Borrowers (July 2016, revised August 2018). Procurement documents advertised in national newspapers and uploaded on KP Public Procurement Regulatory Authority (KPPRA) websites. Standard Bidding Documents used for works, goods, and services. Complaints managed through formal structure with transparent publication on KPPRA website.
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Results Framework with 3 PDO indicators and 13 intermediate results indicators (6 are DLIs). Third-Party Verification Agent (TPVA) conducts semi-annual verification of DLI achievement. Quarterly progress reports from OSU. Semi-annual verification reports from TPVA. Quarterly Program Monitoring meetings. Implementation support missions at least twice yearly. M&E responsibility with Finance Department coordinating across implementing agencies.

identified_risks.risk_description: Recent merger of former FATA into larger KP province creates integration challenges. Political instability and governance transitions could affect implementation continuity.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Ongoing World Bank engagement through Governance and Policy Projects (P156410 and P156412) supporting policy dialog on integration and inclusion. Reform Working Group provides inter-agency coordination. Gradual implementation over 5-year period.

identified_risks.risk_description: KP remains vulnerable to economic shocks, declining forex reserves, and high debt. Federal macro-fiscal policies directly impact provincial finances. IMF program under discussion indicates broader economic challenges.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Gradual implementation over 5-year period to allow resolution of macro-economic shocks. Program designed to increase fiscal space through revenue mobilization, reducing dependence on federal transfers.

identified_risks.risk_description: Low capacity in participating agencies. Frequent turnover of senior officials affects continuity. Limited experience with results-based operations. Insufficient qualified accounting staff despite adequate sanctioned positions.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Continuous support from World Bank Task Team. Augmentation with short-term consultants as needed. Extensive engagement with working-level officials during Program design. Capacity building activities under TA component. Training programs targeting 100+ DDOs in financial management.

identified_risks.risk_description: Systemic weaknesses in Public Financial Management. Outdated processes and fragmented legal/regulatory framework. Incomplete utilization of NFMIS. Uneven implementation of Treasury Single Account. Lack of transparency in managing dedicated funds.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Entire Program subject to audit by Auditor General of Pakistan. Anti-Corruption Protocol implemented. Regular support from Financial Management Specialist. DLI 6 monitors NFMIS implementation. Program Action Plan includes specific fiduciary mitigation measures. Internal audit cells established in 11 line departments. Integrated Fiduciary Systems Assessment confirms existing systems sufficient with mitigation measures.

identified_risks.risk_description: Large number of participating institutions creates coordination challenges. Potential resistance from stakeholders affected by reforms (e.g., removal of unapproved projects from ADP, taxation in PATA/FATA areas, property tax increases affecting slum dwellers).
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Reform Working Group supports inter-agency coordination. Communication campaigns on tax compliance and benefits of reforms. Citizen Integration Forum engages 19 civil society organizations. Tax facilitation centers serve as citizen engagement and grievance redress mechanism. Social risk assessment for PATA and former FATA districts. Consideration of vulnerable communities in UIPT valuation revisions.

identified_risks.risk_description: Policy implementation risks related to consistent policies and institutional/behavioral changes required for targeted results.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Program builds on Government's own PFMRS strategy with strong ownership. Extensive policy dialog between World Bank and GoKP over 3 years. Government has already taken steps through legislation (Local Government Act 2013) and policy notifications.

identified_risks.risk_description: Complexity of integrating multiple tax authority databases. Limited GoKP technical capacity for ICT solutions. Business process automation challenges.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: TA component provides technical assistance for database integration and e-government functionality. Business process mapping and reengineering support. Change management strategy and training. Engagement of KPITB and external consultants for ICT implementation.

identified_risks.risk_description: E-waste from IT equipment procurement (servers, computers, printers, scanners, modems, routers). Potential disproportionate impact on slum dwellers from UIPT revisions. Risk of resistance if unapproved projects removed from ADP.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Program Action Plan requires mechanism for proper e-waste management. ESSA assessment confirms low environmental and social risks. Vulnerable groups consideration in UIPT valuation processes. Communication on benefits of prioritizing implementation-ready projects. Gender analysis of GSTS and property tax impacts on women.

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Multiple mechanisms: (1) Tax facilitation centers serve as citizen engagement and grievance redress platform for tax-related issues; (2) Existing Citizen Integration Forum with 19 civil society organizations and accountability institutions (Right to Public Services Commission, Right to Information Commission, Ehtesab Commission, Peshawar High Court); (3) World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) for complaints about Bank-supported operations; (4) World Bank Inspection Panel for complaints about Bank non-compliance with policies; (5) KPPRA complaint management system for procurement-related grievances with formal structure and transparent publication of decisions.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Program includes gender analysis and gender-sensitive planning. GoKP Women's Empowerment Policy of 2015 provides framework. Program will support: (1) Gender-sensitive planning and budgeting to ensure better allocation of resources in education, health, social services, agriculture; (2) Collection of gender-related Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) across 32 departments in service delivery to women; (3) Analysis of impact of GSTS and property taxes on women to inform gender-responsive policy making; (4) Output-based budgeting departments with measurable gender KPIs reporting number of females reached by services (target: 32% of OBB departments by end of program).
vulnerable_groups: Slum dwellers and low-income property owners (affected by UIPT revisions)
Residents of PATA and former FATA districts (affected by taxation expansion)
Women (gender disparities in human development outcomes, labor force participation only 20.1%)
Children (40% below age 5 stunted, 36% out of school)
Poor and those just above poverty line (need targeted safety nets)
Disadvantaged actors potentially affected by removal of unapproved projects from ADP

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Program addresses conflict sensitivity through: (1) Integration of former FATA into KP - Program supports multi-year investment plan for former FATA districts and infrastructure survey based on inclusion and equity; (2) Ongoing World Bank engagement through Governance and Policy Projects supporting policy dialog on integration and inclusion in merged areas; (3) Social risk assessment for PATA and former FATA districts to avoid crises from across-the-board taxation; (4) Communication campaigns on tax compliance and benefits of reforms; (5) Consideration of vulnerable communities in policy implementation; (6) Gradual implementation allowing time for stabilization.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: KP has transitioned out of vulnerability and crises over past 8 years with substantial poverty reduction (from 73.8% in FY02 to 27% in FY14). However, parts of KP remain vulnerable to crises. Key fragility factors: (1) Integration of former FATA (one of poorest regions) creates fiscal challenges and development needs; (2) FATA expected to retain tax-exempt status for 5 years, affecting KP's fiscal situation; (3) Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Tribal Districts Transition Framework has US$726M funding gap (nearly 3/4 of financing requirement); (4) Low human development outcomes (40% child stunting, 36% out of school); (5) High vulnerability to climate change and hydro-meteorological disasters; (6) Dependence on federal transfers (86% of provincial revenue in FY17); (7) Limited domestic revenue mobilization capacity.
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Program does not directly address security but supports institutional strengthening and revenue mobilization to improve government capacity and fiscal space for development. KP's transition from conflict/crisis is ongoing. Program supports: (1) Strengthening government institutions and public financial management; (2) Improving service delivery through better resource allocation; (3) Reducing dependence on federal transfers through own-source revenue mobilization; (4) Building institutional capacity for sustainable development.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: no - Program does not involve physical works, land acquisition, or involuntary resettlement. No displacement or refugee-related issues identified. Program focuses on institutional and financial management reforms.
